message:
test(conformance): self-spawning live-oracle integration (br-frankenredis-5pnv)

Promotes the four previously `#[ignore]`-d live-oracle tests to run
automatically when the vendored upstream redis-server binary is
available. Replaces the external "needs localhost:6379" assumption
with a Drop-guarded VendoredRedis handle that:

- Locates the binary under HarnessConfig::default_paths().oracle_root
  (expected at `<oracle_root>/src/redis-server`).
- Picks a free localhost port via a probe TcpListener and spawns the
  server with --daemonize no, --appendonly no, --save "", and
  --protected-mode no, rooted in a unique tmp --dir.
- Waits up to 3 seconds for the port to accept TCP connections.
- Returns None on any failure (missing binary, spawn error, timeout);
  tests treat None as Skipped with a stderr log rather than failing.
- Kills and reaps the child on Drop.

Two env-var knobs for CI flexibility:
  FR_CONFORMANCE_SKIP_LIVE_ORACLE=1   force skip (silent)
  FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1 opt in to the mismatch gate

Default: tests spawn the oracle (when binary present), print the
differential report to stderr for visibility, but only assert
`report.total > 0` — not `report.total == report.passed`. This ships
the infrastructure without tripping on pre-existing divergences from
frankenredis-fcjh (replication stubs), frankenredis-jqgv (WAIT/WAITAOF
stubs), and similar open beads. STRICT mode enforces the full
byte-for-byte gate and is what post-stub-landing work should run.

Transport errors ("redis did not reply before timeout" on adversarial
RESP) are handled via a shared `run_live_diff_tolerant` helper that
preserves the same default-tolerant / STRICT-strict contract.

Acceptance:
  cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib live_redis_  → 4 passed / 0 failed
  cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  → exit 0
  cargo test --workspace --all-targets  → only remaining failure is
    tcp_waitaof_* (tracked under frankenredis-jqgv).

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>
